headline: Georgia seeks redemption against Notre Dame in South Bend
subhead: Two ranked teams in flux collide with playoff positioning on the line.

xFactor.why: Georgia's defense must prove the Colorado collapse was circumstantial by containing Notre Dame's ground game and limiting explosive plays.

keyMatchup: Georgia's front four against Notre Dame's offensive line will determine whether the Bulldogs can generate interior pressure without selling out to the pass.
body: Georgia arrives in South Bend wounded after surrendering 42 points to Colorado, a result that exposed both run defense lapses and secondary communication breakdowns. The Bulldogs cannot afford another offensive stagnation—their last two games show scoring droughts in the fourth quarter—against a Notre Dame team that just rolled Arkansas with methodical, ball-control football.

Notre Dame's 35-10 win over Arkansas revealed a program trending toward stability under pressure, though the Irish have yet to face an opponent with Georgia's pedigree or defensive talent. The deciding factor may be Georgia's ability to establish run balance and keep drives alive; if the Bulldogs stall in the red zone again, Notre Dame's defense will feel emboldened to tighten coverage and force uncomfortable third-and-longs.
